summary: The preferred laser for TMJ adhesion lysis during arthroscopic procedures is typically a Holmium-yttrium aluminum garnet (Ho-YAG) laser because of its precise operation and reduced heat generation which minimizes tissue damage around the intricate joint structure. This consistency across multiple sources supports continuing use of A as indicated by Agent1’s initial response based largely upon these same attributes specific to treating such an anatomically complex region non-invasively yet effectively through targeted energy delivery methods provided uniquely by modern medical technologies including advanced surgical lasers designed explicitly for orthopedic applications involving joints prone to scarring or fibrosis post-trauma or surgery. To summarize concisely while retaining core elements from both previous responses: For TMJ adhesion release via arthrosopic means, preference leans towards using a Ho-YAG laser owing primarily to
